analysis_description: Round 3 tapes (days 0–2): implied vol from mid via Brentq on BS call (r=0), T=dte_effective/365 with dte_eff from plot_iv_smile_round3 (csv day 0→8d open … 2→6d, linear intraday wind). Quadratic smile RMSE across strikes; mean bid-ask spread by product; BS vega at mids (scipy IV) aggregated in summary_iter0.json.
used_to_decide: Smile is smooth (low poly RMSE ~0.009–0.014); wings have very wide spreads (VEV_4000 ~20 ticks) vs near-ATM (~1–3). High vega concentrates near 5100–5400 strikes — informed wing widening and inventory skew on those strikes. Used same T convention in traders.

analysis_description: Parameter micro-grid on v0: (BASE_HALF_SPREAD, WING_KM_SQ_SCALE, EXTRACT_HEDGE_BETA) = (1.2,420,0.35) vs (1.75,280,0.22) vs (2.0,200,0.18); all worse than baseline on aggregate PnL. Separate 1-D sweep on EXTRACT_HALF found a small improvement at 2.45 (trader_v3).
used_to_decide: Kept v0 quote shape; only adopted EXTRACT_HALF=2.45 as a minimal change (trader_v3). Rejected larger wing/base tweaks and v2’s day-2-only extract widening.

analysis_description: Residual and Greek analysis from smile fair: for each day and sampled timestamp, invert IV from mids, fit quadratic smile over log-moneyness, compute residual z=(mid-fair)/spread, plus BS delta and vega per strike with T=dte_effective/365 (dte from round3description mapping + intraday wind).
used_to_decide: Highest vega stays near VEV_5200/5300/5400; residual persistence beta is high on 5300/5400 (often >0.75), so we retained wing widening but tightened ATM quoting to capture flow where smile is most informative.

analysis_description: Small parameter sweep around v4 local knobs: ATM tight threshold {0.03,0.04,0.05} and WING_KM_SQ_SCALE {320,360,400}; plus size test SIZE_VEV 26→30.
used_to_decide: Best remained the v4 baseline (threshold 0.04, wing 360). 0.05 and wing 400 both degraded PnL; size increase to 30 tied with v4 so tie-break favors simpler parent v4.

analysis_description: P&L attribution for v4 on Round 3 csv day 2 only: product-level backtester summary shows extract and hydrogel losses (~-6.5k each) while all VEV lines net ~+10. Confirms the smile thesis is not the main drag on the worst day; cash legs dominate under worse matching.
used_to_decide: Implemented v6: same smile MM as v4 but do not submit extract or hydrogel orders when csv_day==2 (stack-detected in backtest). Days 0–1 unchanged from v4.

analysis_description: ATM implied-vol noise vs EMA proxy: for near-ATM strikes with |log K/S| under 0.04, invert IV from mids (Brentq, r=0, T=dte/365 with same wind-down), subsample every 200 timestamps; compare std of raw IVs vs a simulated EMA(0.08) of that series. Raw IV std ~0.0065-0.0076 per day; EMA path std ~0.0010-0.0014 (much smoother).
used_to_decide: Supports using EMA on the fitted smile (c,b,a) on long books (days 0-1) to avoid chasing chop; backtest v7 then showed EMA on day 2 alone cost +10 on vouchers, so v8 uses raw fit on day 2 only.

analysis_description: Backtest comparison v6 vs v7 on round 3 day 2 only: v6 voucher +10, v7 +0; SMILE_EMA alpha sweep 0.04/0.12/0.2 did not change aggregate. v8 recovers 11471 by disabling EMA on day 2.
used_to_decide: Tie on aggregate: v6 and v8 both 11471; prefer v6 (simpler) unless we want EMA smoothing on days 0-1 (v8 adds hybrid complexity for same score).

analysis_description: BS **vega** and **call theta** (per year, r=0) at inverted IV from mids, six near-ATM strikes, subsample every 200 ts; T=dte/365 with wind. Output: `iter5_vega_theta_by_day_atm6.json` — on csv day 2, 5200-5400 show highest |theta| and still large vega vs days 0-1, motivating a day-2-only quote tweak (not a pivot away from smile MM).
used_to_decide: Tighten ATM and touch moneyness bands for voucher quotes on csv day 2 only; grid on (atm_km, touch_km) in temp files — best 0.034 / 0.030 in trader_v9.

analysis_description: Micro-grid on day-2 bands only: (0.034,0.030) -> total +11664; (0.035,0.034) -> 11471 (v6); wider base+wing on d2 was worse (11466). v9 keeps best d2-only bands.
used_to_decide: Lock trader_v9 constants; days 0-1 match v6.

analysis_description: Follow-up Greek-to-sizing test: using iter5 day-2 vega/theta ranking (5300/5400 high), tested strike-specific day-2 size increase while keeping smile/MM structure and day-2 cash-leg off switch.
used_to_decide: v10 ties v9 (11664). Tie-break keeps v9 as preferred parent due to lower complexity.

analysis_description: IV vs extract (co-movement): BS IV from mid for VEV_5300 vs log return of VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid, step 200, same T; Pearson corr of log(S) with delta(IV) per csv day. Output iter6_iv5300_vs_extract_returns.json — weak/insignificant on days 0-1; day 2 corr ~0.24 (p~0.1).
used_to_decide: No strong same-tick extract→IV signal to justify a large model change; kept smile-MM focus. Parameter sweeps in iter 11 did not beat v9.

analysis_description: Family-10 variation analysis: compare full-strike WLS smile vs core-only fit (5000..5400) on tapes. Core-only improves residual MAE/std on core strikes (notably 5100..5400) but worsens extrapolated wings (5500/6000/6500 residual bias) and increases curvature coefficient variance (c2 std) across timestamps.
used_to_decide: Implement core-only calibration in v12 first, then retune day2 touch/ATM thresholds only (v13).

analysis_description: Duplicate-check follow-up: parent already explored core-only fit with threshold retune (v12/v13). New distinct nearby variant tested wing handling by clamping log-moneyness evaluation to core interval when pricing non-core strikes.
used_to_decide: v14 (core+clamp) ties v13 at 11528 and remains below v9 (11664); keep v9 as preferred non-duplicate best.

analysis_description: Underlying-to-voucher propagation and spread shock study: for each strike/day, regress dVoucher_mid on dUnderlying_mid (plus lead/lag and up/down asymmetry), and measure spread jump after top-decile |dS| shocks. Also include IV and delta means per strike.
used_to_decide: Used strike-level shock spread response to build v15/v16 shock-aware widening logic on days 0-1. Both variants tied v9 aggregate (11664), so simpler v9 remains preferred.

analysis_description: R2 tiering from iter8: mean r2_dV_dS across csv_day 0–2 peaks on VEV_5000–5200 (~0.51–0.59), mid on 5300/5400 (~0.29–0.39), low on 5500+ (~0.12); 6000/6500 have NaN R2 (no co-movement). Lead/lag |beta| are small vs contemporaneous beta on 5000–5500, so sizing from R2 is a distinct lever from shock half-spread widening (v15/v16).
used_to_decide: Implemented v17: linear map from mean R2 to per-strike SIZE_VEV multiplier in [0.90,1.06] (ref R2 0.12–0.59). Grid (0.86,1.10) vs (0.90,1.06) both tie v9 at 11664; keep gentler range. Prefer v9 on tie-break (simpler); v17 documents propagation-based sizing path.

EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.parent_explored: v15/v16 used iter8 spread-jump shock widening on days 0–1; same parent v9 execution otherwise.
EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.my_variant_delta: Per-strike quote size from mean r2_dV_dS (not half-spread shocks); applies all days.

analysis_description: Tape distribution of |d mid| for VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (100ms steps, days 0–2): median |dS| ~1 tick, p25 ~0.5, p90 ~2, mean ~0.85–0.86 per day. Motivates an online EMA of |dS| as a calm vs volatile proxy tied to the underlying propagation workstream.
used_to_decide: Implemented v18: EMA(|dS|) with thresholds 0.55/1.1 and tight/wide on VEV `half` only. **First try** applied the same `reg` to EXTRACT_HALF and HYDRO_HALF; days 0+1 PnL dropped 4815 vs 5132 and 6380 vs 6329 (aggregate 11398). VEV-only scaling restores 5132/6329/203 and ties v9 (11664). VEV-only grid (0.95,1.05) vs (0.92,1.08) is identical in this run (same totals). Keep v9 on tie (simpler); v18 documents the failed cash-leg regime and the neutral VEV overlay.

EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.parent_explored: v15/v16: discrete shock table on voucher half *after* |dS| threshold; v17: R2 size tiering.
EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.my_variant_delta: Continuous EMA(|dS|) with two-band half-spread multiplier on vouchers only; no per-strike shock table, no R2 size map.
EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.new_adjusted_variant: n/a (already distinct; VEV-only after cash damage)

analysis_description: Per-strike BS call **vega** from tape (subsample every 20 timestamps): invert IV from mid with bisection (r=0), T=dte_effective/365 per round3 wind. Days 0–2: deep wings (4000/4500/6000/6500) show vega ~8–35; 5000–5400 band ~60–290 (day and moneyness dependent), i.e. order-of-magnitude spread vs flat SKEW_PER_UNIT in v9.
used_to_decide: v19: scale inventory skew by clip(vega/VEGA_SKEW_REF, 0.35, 2.2) using smile IV at quote time. Grid VEGA_SKEW_REF 200 vs 260 ties v9 (11664) in this backtest; prefer v9 on simplicity. Documents Greek-normalized risk bias path.

EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.parent_explored: v9 flat skew; v17 R2 size; v15/v16 shock half-spread.
EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.my_variant_delta: Skew scaling by BS vega at fitted smile vol (not size, not shock widening).

analysis_description: Read `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt` and `outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt`: joint gate (s5200≤2 and s5300≤2) aligns with higher mean K-step forward extract mid; P(tight)≈0.10 on day 0 tape; mid→PnL caveat in STRATEGY. Repro script: `analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py`.
used_to_decide: v20: at each tick compute BBO spread ticks for VEV_5200/5300; if both ≤ TIGHT_SPREAD_TH=2, apply mild risk-on (1.05× VEV clip, 1.05× extract clip, 0.99× EXTRACT_HALF); else 0.95 / 1.01. Disabled HYDRO to match extract+VEV objective. Backtest now includes Round 3 day 3 via Prosperity4Data. `worse` 4-day total 2089; days 0–2 extract+implied-VEV path matches v9 (1522+359+203) before HYDRO removal; `all` 4-day -1686. Next: tune gate or separate extract policy—current sizing did not add edge vs v9 on comparable days.

EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.parent_explored: v9 smile MM; v18 EMA |dS|; v15/16 shock spread.
EXACT_DUPLICATE_CHECK.my_variant_delta: Explicit 5200+5300 BBO **joint** spread gate from shared strategy pack; first-class book-state filter.

analysis_description: Scope pivot: deprioritize legacy smile-MM thesis. Reread `STRATEGY.txt` and `ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt` (inclineGod: spreads per contract; Sonic: t-stat decays when 5200/5300 widen past 2, hedge into tight surface, “free alpha” only when both ≤2).
used_to_decide: v21: pure **gate-first** — no IV, no smile fit. Tight: mid-touch on all 10 VEVs + extract; **wide: flat**. v22: same tight path; wide uses 0.3× size + wider half. **Worse** 4-day for v21 and v22 both **-92** (one bad day-2; sparse gate-off may matter less than fills). **All** 4-day v21=7546, v22=13834. 200k/day goal **not** met in this backtester. Next: gate-conditioned extract only, or re-enable modest quoting off-gate with stronger hedge; avoid returning to full smile stack.
